rule: Never write any of the 15 validator-penalized words (fast/quick/slow/good/bad/poor/user-friendly/easy/simple/secure/safe/scalable/flexible/performant/efficient) anywhere in the PRD, including hyphenated idioms and SQL comments.
Never resolve, remove, or reword a [DECISION: ...] marker; open decisions route to the human queue only.
Any fold-in that edits a REQ must back-propagate to earlier-written dependents: design/ docs, §11 scope bullets, schema comments, roadmap task hints, and cross-referenced REQs.

Money-path wording is invariant-bearing: paired rows + pair_id, INTEGER micro-cents with display-time rounding only, skim-at-consumption, control totals. Rewrites may rephrase around these but never restate the math loosely.
